LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT GARY WEGER'S MOTION FROM RELIEF FROM DEFAULT ENTRY- THE MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 473. DEFENDANT PROVIDED A PROPOSED ANSWER WITH THIS MOTION. DEFENDANT FILED THIS MOTION IN A TIMELY MANNER. DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THE DEFAULT IN APRIL 2013 AND FILED THE MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT A MONTH LATER. DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATES EXCUSABLE NEGLECT IN FAILING TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. ONLY SLIGHT EVIDENCE IS NECESSARY FOR RELIEF BECAUSE PLAINTIFF IS NOT PREJUDICED. SEE GENERALLY MINK V. SUPERIOR COURT, 2 CAL. APP. 4TH 1338, 343 (1992). IN ADDITION, THERE IS A POLICY TO HEAR CASES ON THE MERITS. THE REQUEST FOR CONDITIONS TO GRANT THIS MOTION IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion From Relief From Default Entry
Off Calendar Jul-03-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DONALD A. THOMPSON OF HARVEY SISKIND LLP, TEL. NO. 354-0100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARIA A. BOURIN OF THE DOLAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 421-2800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT GARY WEGER'S Motion From Relief From Default Entry- The motion for relief from default is granted pursuant to CCP 473. Defendant provided a proposed answer with this motion. Defendant filed this motion in a timely manner. Defendant learned of the default in April 2013 and filed the motion for relief from default a month later. Defendant demonstrates excusable neglect in failing to respond to the complaint. Only slight evidence is necessary for relief because plaintiff is not prejudiced. See generally Mink v. Superior Court, 2 Cal. App. 4th 1338, 343 (1992). In addition, there is a policy to hear cases on the merits. The request for conditions to grant this motion is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion From Relief From Default Entry
Off Calendar Jun-05-2013 Continued To Jul-03-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GARY WEGER's Motion From Relief From Default Entry IS Continued to July 3, 2013 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 7, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: L. Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
